name: Valley Wide
description_short: Valley Wide Federal Credit Union is a member-owned financial cooperative with a branch location in Vernal, Utah, offering credit union services to its members.
description_long: Valley Wide Federal Credit Union operates as a federally chartered, member-owned credit union serving communities in Utah. As a not-for-profit financial institution, it is regulated by the National Credit Union Administration (NCUA) and provides member-focused financial services typical of the credit union model. The organization maintains at least one physical branch location in Vernal, Utah, where members can access in-person banking services. The credit union is listed through the National Credit Union Service Organization (NCUSO) directory, which helps consumers locate credit union branches and services. Valley Wide Federal Credit Union, like other credit unions, operates on a cooperative basis where members are part-owners of the institution rather than serving external shareholders. This structure typically allows credit unions to offer competitive rates and member-centric policies. However, the available online information is limited, with the NCUSO directory primarily providing branch location details rather than comprehensive service descriptions. Prospective members should contact the Vernal branch directly or seek additional information about specific products, membership eligibility requirements, and current rates to make fully informed decisions.
services: In-person banking services at Vernal branch
Credit union membership for eligible individuals
Branch location in Vernal, UT with physical address access
pros: Federal credit union status means NCUA insurance protection for member deposits
Member-owned, not-for-profit structure typically results in competitive rates and lower fees
Physical branch location in Vernal, Utah allows in-person service and relationship banking
NCUSO directory listing provides transparency and accessibility information
Credit union model prioritizes member benefits over shareholder profits
cons: Limited online presence—minimal service details available on directory listing
Single branch location may restrict accessibility for members outside Vernal area
Website content does not clearly specify membership eligibility or account types offered
No information available about fees, rates, or specific loan products
Contact information is basic; no online banking portal details provided
best_for: Utah residents in or near Vernal seeking local credit union banking
Members who prefer face-to-face banking and community-focused financial institutions
Consumers looking for potential lower fees and competitive rates through member-owned structure
diagnosis: Valley Wide Federal Credit Union is best suited for Utah residents in the Vernal area seeking membership in a community-based, not-for-profit financial cooperative with NCUA protection. The main caveat is that publicly available information is extremely limited—prospective members must contact the branch directly to understand membership requirements, specific products offered, rates, and fees, as the directory listing does not provide these details.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
